txt: Dewhurst, Robert, fellow of University Coll. 1559, B.A. 30 Jan., 1562-3, M.A. 30 June, 1565.

Usher, Henry, matric. at Cambridge 2 May, 1567, B.A. from Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1569-70; incorporated 1 July, 1572; M.A. from University Coll. 11 July, 1572; treasurer of Christ Church, Dublin, 1573, and archdeacon of Dublin 1580; the first fellow of Trinity Coll., Dublin, archdeacon of Dublin, archbishop of Armagh 1595, until his death 2 April, 1613, buried in St. Peter's Drogheda. See Ath. ii. 852; & Cotton's Fasti Ecc. Hib. iii. 20.

Glynn, Richard, of co. Carnarvon, arm. Commoner of University Coll. in or before 1572, aged 23; B.A. 28 Jan., 1573-4, M.A. 5 June, 1576 (son of William, of Glyn Llyvon, co. Carnarvon), rector of Trawsfynydd, co. Merioneth, 1582, of Llanvaethle, Anglesey, 1587, rector of Llantrissaint, canon of St. Asaph 1598-1617, canon and archdeacon of Bangor 1613, until his death 9 Sept. 1617, See Foster's Index Eccl.

Glynn, Owen, of co. Carnarvon, arm. Commoner of University Coll. in or before 1572, aged 20; B.A. 28 Jan., 1573-4, M.A. 20 June, 1576 (incorporated at Cambridge 1581), B.D. 6 May, 1583, D.D. 14 July, 1590, rector of Litchborough, Northants, 1580, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Mo(o)re, John, commoner of University Coll. in or before 1572; perhaps B.A. 16 Dec., 1573, M.A. 2 July, 1576, rector of Knaptoft, co. Leicester, 1586-1619, succeeded by another of the same names, perhaps John 1617. See Ath. ii. 193; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Fitz, John (Fyts), commoner of University Coll. in or before 1572.

T(h)omson, Giles, of London, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19, exhibitioner 1571, B.A. 5 July, 1575, M.A. 4 July, 1578; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1580 (perhaps incorporated at Cambridge 1581), proctor 1586, B.D. 21 March, 1589-90, D.D. 8 July, 1602; admitted to Merchant Taylors' school 3 March, 1563-4 (son of Giles, a grocer, deceased); one of the translators of the Bible, chaplain to the queen, canon residentiary of Hereford 1584, rector of Pembridge, co. Hereford, 1592, dean of Windsor 1602, and registrar of the garter, bishop of Gloucester 1611, until his death 14 June, 1612. See Ath. ii. 850; Robinson, i. 5; Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 197; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Stonehowse, (Sir) William (or Stockhouse), of London, gen. fil. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18; of Radley, Berks, admitted to Merchant Taylors' school 1572 (s. George, esq.); student of Inner Temple 1574; licenced 7 June, 1592, to marry Elizabeth, daughter of John Powell, of Fulham, Middlesex, gent.; created a baronet 7 May, 1628; father of John 1617, and probably of the next; died 5 Feb., 1631. See Robinson, i. 19; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Leighe, (Sir) Oliph, of Surrey, gen. fil. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 14; student of Lincoln's Inn 1576, admitted to Merchant Taylors' school 1572 (s. John of Addington, Surrey); born 21 Nov., 1559, fitted out an expedition to Guiana circa 1604, knighted by Q. Elizabeth 1604; died 14 March, 1611-12, buried at Addington, Surrey; father of Francis 1607. See Robinson, i. 19; Purchas' Pilgrims, ii.; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Crispe, John (Crypse), (s. Thomas, of Marshfield), co. Gloucester, pleb. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 20; B.A. 5 July, 1575, M.A. 5 May, 1578 (as James), rector of Lydiard Milicent, Wilts, 1579, made his will 13 Aug., 1603, proved 14 Aug., 1604. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Leigh, Philip (Lee), of Cheshire, gen. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19; B.A. 31 Jan., 1576-7, M.A. 8 May, 1579, vicar of Great Sandall, Yorks, 1584, and of Ellesmere, Salop, 1588, rector of Hotham, Yorks, 1606, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Kekewiche, Gregory (or Keckwiche), of Cornwall, gen. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 17; not named in pedigree; perhaps intended for George, son and heir of George, of Catchfrench; baptised at Menheniot 14 Aug., 1556. See Vivian's Cornwall.

Locket, William, of Somerset, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18; B.A. 11 May, 1575, M.A. 11 March, 1577-8, rector of Warmwell 1576, and of Corscombe, Dorset, 1580; father of Stephen 1625, and of William 1611. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Kisbie, Robert, of Berks, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 15; B.A. 13 April, 1579, M.A. 28 May, 1582, vicar of Abingdon St. Helen, Berks, 1597-1624, and also of St. Nicholas 1620-5. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Batson, Henry, of co. Lancaster, pleb. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 20; B.A. 3 March, 1574-5, canon of Exeter 1580, rector of Eggesford, Devon, 1583. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Braddyll, John, of co. Lancaster, gent. University Coll., matric-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 17; of Brasenose Coll. 1575, B.A. 16 Feb., 1576-7, a student of Gray's Inn 1572. See Foster's Gray's Inn Admissions.

Grenville, Barnard, of Cornwall, arm. University Coll., matric. entry under date 1574, aged 15; if this be Sir Barnard, of Stowe, his father Sir Richard who was born about 1543 must have married early in his teens.

Aston, William, of co. Stafford, mil. fil. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 1574, aged 19; perhaps of Milwich, co. Stafford, buried at Tixall 6 May, 1628, and brother of Devereux. See O.H.S., i.
Wardroper, Walter, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19, B.A. 5 July, 1575, fellow, M.A. 30 April, 1579; admon. at Oxford 6 Oct., 1586.
Tuke, Edmund, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 17, B.A. 28 Feb., 1578-9, M.A. 6 July, 1583, as Edward.
Bolt, Thomas (or Belt), of co. Stafford, gent. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18; inventory at Oxford 16 May, 1578.
Osbaldeston, Francis (Osbolston), of co. Warwick, gent. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 16, B.A. 12 July, 1578.
Darbyshire, (Robert), of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 1574; perhaps B.A. 13 March, 1575-6, as Robert.
Clode, Richard, of co. Somerset, pleb. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 17; B.A. 12 June, 1578.
Warren, Roger, of Devon, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 20, B.A. 5 July, 1575.
Browne, Robert, of co, Somerset, pleb. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 15745-5, aged 20.
Gunter, Thomas, of London, gent. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18.
Norris, John, of Cheshire, gent. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19.
Dewe, Henry, of London, pleb. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 15.
Newman, William, of Bucks, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5.
Daly, —, of co. Meath, Ireland, pleb. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 1574.

Holforde, Christopher, of Cheshire, arm. University Coll., matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 18; student of Gray's Inn 1577, licenced 20 Dec., 1581, to marry Mary Vynar, of St. Stephen, Coleman Street, spinster. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster, & Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Wyatt, William, of co. Worcester, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1589, as son and heir of Ralph, of Worcester. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Daniel, Peter, of Cheshire, arm. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 16; B.A. 12 June, 1578, of Gray's Inn 1578, father of the next named and of Richard 1602. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Glover, (—), of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 20.
Gerard, Thomas, arm. University Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 13; perhaps specially admitted to Gray's Inn 25 Nov., 1577. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Molyneux, Richard, arm. University Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18.

War(r)en, (Sir) Edward, of Cheshire, arm. fil. University Coll., matric. entry under date 2 July. 1578, aged 15, B.A. 23 Jan., 1580-1, M.A. 3 July, 1582; of Poynton and Stockport, Cheshire, high sheriff 1598, (s. of John), baptised 9 April, 1563, M.P. Liverpool 1588-9, knighted in Ireland 22 July, 1599; died 12 Nov., 1609; brother of Laurence. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Glynne, (Sir) William, of co. Carnarvon. University Coll., matric. entry under date 2 July, 1578, aged 12 (as arm.); of Glyn Llyvon, co. Carnarvon, student of Lincoln's Inn 1586 (s. Thomas), knighted in Dublin Castle 7 June, 1606, by Sir Arthur Chichester, lord deputy, M.P. co. Anglesea 1593, then of Llanvrog, co. Anglesey; father of Sir John 1621. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Leycester, (Sir) George, of Cheshire, arm. fil. University Coll., matric. entry under date 2 July, 1578, aged 13; of Toft, Cheshire, sheriff 1602-3, knighted 6 Aug., 1599, at Dublin, by the Earl of Essex, M.P. Thirsk 1597; buried 4 April, 1612; brother of Ralph 1578. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Rediche, Alexander, of co. Lancaster, arm. University Coll., matric. entry under date 2 July, 1578, aged 14; student of Gray's Inn 1580. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Leycester, Ralph, of Cheshire, arm. University Coll., matric. entry under date 2 July, 1578, aged 16; brother of George 1578.
Winnington, Thomas (Wynnington), of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 2 July, 1578, aged 20.
Hodgson, Henry (Hodgeson), of London, gent. University Coll., matric. entry under date 2 July, 1578, aged 19.
Hodgshon, Marmaduke, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 2 July, 1578, aged 19.
Malthus, William, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date 2 July, 1578, aged 22.

Jenninges, John, of Somerset, gen. University Coll., matric. entry under date circa (? 1580), aged 13; student of Middle Temple 1585, as 2nd son of Ralph, of Churchill, Somerset, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Robertes, Morris, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 19; B.A. 23 Feb., 1581-2, M.A. 30 June, 1585, rector of Penmorfa, co. Carnarvon, 1590. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jennings, Thomas, of Somerset, gen. University Coll., matric. entry under date circa (? 1580), aged 14.

Bostocke, Thomas, of Berks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 15; B.A. 4 July, 1593, chaplain of New Coll., M.A. 3 April, 1596 (student of the Middle Temple 1590, formerly of New Inn, as son and heir of Anthony, of Abingdon, Berks, esquire), one of these names vicar of Acton, Cheshire, 1601. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Eatonn, Richard, of co. Chester, pleb. University Coll., matric. 2 March, 1581-2, aged 18; B.A. from Lincoln Coll., 1 Feb., 1585-6, M.A. 9 July, 1589, B.D. 5 July, 1599, vicar of Gt. Budworth, Cheshire, 1604, canon of Lichfield 1607; father of Theophilus, governor of New Haven, and of Samuel, who died 1665. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hesilrige, (Sir) Thomas, of co. Leicester, arm. fil. University Coll., matric. 17 March, 1581-2, aged 17; of Nosely, co. Leicester, high sheriff 1612 (s. Thomas), knighted 19 June, 1608, created a baronet 21 Aug., 1622, M.P. co. Leicester, 1614, 1624-5; died 11 Jan., 1629, aged 66; father of Donald. See Foster's Baronetage.

Watmowghe, Hugh, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19, B.A. 29 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 6 July, 1586, B.D. 27 June, 1594, licenced to preach 18 April, 1597; rector of Thornton-in-Craven, Yorks, 1599, and of Bury, co. Lancaster, 1608; father of Timothy 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Massingberd, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, gent. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 18; of Braytoft Hall, co. Lincoln; bar.-at-law, of the Inner Temple, 1593, as of Saltfleetby, co. Lincoln (1s. Thomas), died at Gumby 5 Nov., 1636; grandfather of the next-named. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Waterhous, David, of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1593, bencher 1605; of Shibden, Yorks, coroner in king's bench, M.P. Aldborough 1588-9, Berwick-upon-Tweed 1601, perhaps father of John 1611. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Pennyston, Thomas, of Oxon, arm. University Coll., matric. entry 1 Dec., 1581, aged 13; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1595, as of Deane, Oxon, junior (s. Thomas, of Beaconsfield, Bucks); father of Thomas next-named. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Horsman, Gilbert, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17; B.A. 2 July, 1585, sup. for M.A. 13 Dec., 1591, B.D. 24 April, 1599; rector of Farthingho, Northants, 1601-2, and of Masham, Yorks, 1603. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Fletcher, William, of co. Derby, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1593, bencher 1607 (as of Makeney, co. Derby, sometime of Clement's Inn, gen.). See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Hodgkins, William (Hotchkin), of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 20; sup. for B.A. 10 Oct., 1583, as Hodgkins; one of these names rector of Sandford, Wilts, 1583, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Flathers, Marmaduke, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 21; B.A. 13 Feb., 1592-3; one Marmaduke Feathers rector of Hemsworth, Yorks, 1620-37. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Colclough, (Sir) Thomas, of Ireland. University Coll., matric. 23 March, 1581-2, aged 17 (as arm.); of Tintern Abbey, knight (created 24 Oct., 1591), died 23 Aug., 1624, father of the next named.
Blount, Richard (Blonte), of co. Stafford, gent. University Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 19 (? B.A. from Balliol Coll. 29 Nov., 1582); fellow of Trinity College 1583.
Palfrey, William, of Somerset, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 18; subscribes as "Pawlffrey," bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1591. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Tatton, Robert, of Cheshire, arm. University Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1581-2, aged 15; of Wythenshawe, Cheshire (s. William); died at Southwark 10 Jan., 1623-4; father of the next.
Rixton, Ralph, of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 18; B.A. from Hart Hall 13 May, 1585, rector of Kingsdon, Somerset, 1588. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Miles, Richard (Myles), University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581; one of these names rector of Llanfrynach, co. Pembroke, 1600. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Downe, William, 'serviens,' of Devon. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19 (subscribes his name more like David than Downe). See O.H.S., xi. 114.
Wolrich, John, of co. Stafford, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19 (as Woldrige), B.A. 22 Feb., 1581-2, M.A. 10 Dec., 1584, as Wolridge.
Bernardus, John, a Moravian. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 28 (as pleb.); B.D. 3 June, 1583. See Fasti, i. 224; & O.H.S., x. 380.
Powell, John, of Salop, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 18; B.A. 6 Feb., 1583-4; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 15 Nov., 1587.
Griffith, Maurice (Gruffythe), of co. Carnarvon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 15, called "Morris Gryffin" in Mat. Reg.
Jordan, John, of Somerset, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 22 Nov., 1585, M.A. 2 July, 1588.
Browne, Richard (? Broome), of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 18; B.A. 31 Oct., 1583, M.A. 28 Jan., 1586-7.
Millington, Thomas (Myllington), of Salop, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19; B.A. 6 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 6 July, 1586.
Prichard, Robert (Prychard), of co. Carnarvon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 2 March, 1581-2, aged 23; B.A. 30 June, 1582.
Pownall, Edmund, of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 20; B.A. from Hart Hall 22 Feb., 1583-4.
Davies, Lancelot (Davys), of co. Flint, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 20; perhaps same man as next.
Rhodes, Abraham (Roodes), of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17, B.A. 23 May, 1584.
Wagstaffe, Edward, of co. Warwick, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 16, B.A. 31 Oct., 1583.
Morgan, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19; B.A. 9 July, 1585.
Turner, William, of co. Lancaster. University Coll., matric. entry 1 Dec., 1581, aged 22, "serviens colleg."
Alcock, John, of Oxon, arm. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 15. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Bostocke, Edmund, of Berks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 15; B.A. 21 Feb., 1586-7.
Welles, Francis, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 20, B.A. 4 Feb., 1582-3.
Wade, William, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17, B.A. 31 Oct., 1583.
Jackson, Edward, of co. Stafford, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1581-2, aged 18.
Jackson, Henry, of co. Stafford, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1581-2, aged 19.
Kempe, Thomas, of co. Warwick, cler. fil. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 24.
Mascall, William, of co. Worcester, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 18.
Palmere, John, of co. Warwick, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1581-2, aged 21.
Wiglesworth, Matthias, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 20.
Davies, Lancelot, of co. Flint, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 22.
Willes, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19.
Butler, Ambrose, of Northants, arm. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17.
Wescombe, John, of Somerset, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 18.
Lawgham, John, of Somerset, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 20.
Pickard, Jeffrey, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19.
Williams, James, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19.
Webb, Antony, of Wilts, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 20.

Temple, (Sir) Thomas, of Bucks, arm. fil. University Coll., matric. 22 June, 1582, aged 16; of Stowe, Bucks, baptised 9 Jan., 1566-7; student of Lincoln's Inn 1584, knighted July, 1603, created a baronet 29 June, 1611 (s. John, of Stowe), M.P. Andover 1588-9, died 1625; father of Thomas next-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Turner, Gregory, of co. Lancaster, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 16; perhaps S.T.P., and rector of Sefton, co. Lancaster; admitted to Gray's Inn 1615, Reg. 136; or B.A. from Pembroke Hall, Cambridge, 1585-6.

Woodwarde, Richard, of co. Warwick, gent. University Coll., matric. 1 Aug., 1582, aged 15; perhaps student of Middle Temple 1591, as 2s. of Thomas, of Butlers Marston, co. Warwick, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Lookar, William, of Hants, gent. University Coll., matric. 16 Nov., 1582, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1595, as of Basingstoke, Hants, late of Lyons Inn, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Davye, Robert, of London, gent. University Coll., matric. 6 April, 1582, aged 17, B.A. 15 July, 1584; fellow Merton Coll. 1584, M.A. 20 Feb., 1588-9 (as Davys), died in Florence. See O.H.S., iv. 273.
Chope, George, of Devon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 30 March, 1582, aged 21; B.A. 29 Oct., 1585, one of these names rector of South Shoebury, Essex, 1608. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Colclough, Leonard, of Ireland, arm. University Coll., matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 14; probably 7th and younger son of Anthony, of Blorton, co. Stafford, and brother of the next named.
Harwood, Thomas, of Kent, gent. University Coll., matric. 4 May, 1582, aged 17; one of these names vicar of Herne, Kent, 1618. See Foster's Index Eccl.; & also Harward.
Cheyney, Walter, of Kent, gent. University Coll., matric. 15 March, 1582-3, aged 18; B.A. 28 Jan., 1586-7, M.A. 21 May, 1590.
Humphrey, Francis (Humphfrei), of co. Carnarvon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 8 Feb., 1582-3, aged 15.
Herbert, William (Harberte), of co. Montgomery, arm. University Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1582, aged 15.
Grange, Richard (Grainge), of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 Nov., 1582, aged 17.
Terry, Robert (Tyrrye), of Ireland, pleb. University Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1582, aged 15.
Harlakinden, Benjamin, of Kent, gent. University Coll., matric. 15 March, 1582-3, aged 18.
Potter, Nisell (or Nizaell), of Kent, arm. University Coll., matric. 4 May, 1582, aged 17.
Hancockes, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. University Coll., matric. 4 May, 1582, aged 18.
Frenche, Peter, of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1582, aged 22.
Davye, William, of London, gent. University Coll., matric. 6 April, 1582, aged 15.

Davis, Samuel, of Bristol, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1583, aged 18; B.A. 12 Feb., 1587-8, M.A. 28 June, 1592, vicar of Bedminster, Somerset, 1592, and of St. Mary Redcliffe in Bristol 1592. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Booth, Edmund (Bouthe), of Cheshire, eq. fil. University Coll., matric. 6 March, 1583-4, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1598, as son of Sir William, of Cheshire, knight, brother of George 1580. See Foster's Gray's Inn Admissions.

Newman, John, of Bucks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1583, aged 17; B.A. 22 Feb., 1586-7, M.A. supd. 18 May, 1590; perhaps rector of Remenham, Berks, 1603; perhaps brother of Henry 1585, and father of John 1618.

Brereton, William, of Cheshire, arm. University Coll., matric. 6 March, 1583-4, aged 15; William (3rd son of William), of Ashley, Cheshire, M.P. 1624-5, died 29 May, 1631, aged 35. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Rutton, Abraham (Rutten), of London, "gen. cond." University Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1583, aged 16; citizen and brewer of London; died mayor of Sandwich, Kent, in 1600; grandfather of Matthias.
Jones, Owen, of co. Carnarv., pleb. University Coll., matric. 13 Dec., 1583, aged 19; called "Johnes" in Mat. Reg.
Davies, Thomas (Davyes), of London, gent. University Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1583, aged 15; B.A. 18 Feb., 1586-7.
Middleton, Richard (Myddletoune), of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. 7 June, 1583, aged 19.
Leigh, William (Leghe), of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. 6 March, 1583-4, aged 26.
Woodwarde, Ezechias, of co. Warwick, gent. University Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1583, aged 15.
Brereton, Richard, of Cheshire, arm. University Coll., matric. 6 March, 1583-4, aged 14.

Wentworthe, Thomas, of Oxon, arm. University Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1584, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1594, treasurer 1621 (s. Peter, of Lillingstone Lovell, Oxon), recorder of Oxford 1607, and M.P. 1604-11, 1614, 1621-2, 1624-5, 1625, 1626, and 3 March, 1627-8, until his death about 1628; brother of Walter. See Ath. ii. 414; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Coxwell, Nathaniel, of co. Gloucester, pleb. University Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1584, aged 12; of the Middle Temple 1594 (as son and heir of John, of Cirencester, co. Gloucester), of Ablington, co. Gloucester, J.P. 1623. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Wentworthe, Walter, of Oxon, arm. University Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1584, aged 15; of Castle Bytham, co. Lincoln (s. Peter, of Lillingstone Lovell); M.P. Tavistock 1601; died 1627; brother of Thomas 1584. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Flasket, Thomas, of London, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Jan., 1584-5, aged 17; B.A. 27 Nov., 1588, M.A. 28 April, 1592, rector of Puddleston, co. Hereford, 1603, vicar of Shinfield, Berks, 1614. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Swetnam, William (Swettnam), of Cheshire, gent. University Coll., matric. 8 May, 1584, aged 20, B.A. 12 Feb., 1587-8, M.A. 11 Dec., 1590; perhaps 3rd son of Thomas and Katherine Swettenham, of Swettenham, Cheshire.
Bower, Raphe (Bowre), of co. Derby, pleb. University Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1584, aged 16; B.A. 17 June, 1588, M.A. 5 July, 1595 (query B.D. from Magdalen Hall 5 May 1614).
Tanckerd, Edmund, of Yorks, arm. University Coll., matric. entry 5 June, 1584, aged 12; 5th son of Thomas, of Boroughbridge, Yorks. See Foster's Yorkshire Collection.
Sc(h)ofeld, Arthur, of co. Lancaster, pleb. University Coll., matric. 12 Feb., 1584-5, aged 21, B.A. 27 Nov., 1588; M.A. from Brasenose Coll. 9 July, 1591.
Newman, Henry, of Bucks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 12 Feb., 1584-5, aged 20; B.A. 27 Nov., 1588, M.A. 6 Feb., 1592-3; perhaps brother of John 1583.
Jauncey, Richard, of co. Worcester, pleb. University Coll., matric. 5 June, 1584, aged 18; B.A. 16 Feb., 1587-8.
Neame, Thomas, of Kent, pleb. University Coll., matric. 8 May, 1584, aged 22, subscribes "gen. cond."
Leigh, Edward (Leghe), of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. 5 June, 1584, aged 19.
Martin, Christopher, of Dorset, gent. University Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1584, aged 14.
Bluet, William, of Cornwall, pleb. University Coll., matric. 19 June, 1584, aged 20.
Devike, Richard, of London, gent. University Coll., matric. 21 Aug., 1584, aged 17.
Warde, James, of Devon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 21.

Kitsone, Richard, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 10 Dec., 1585, aged 18; B.A. 17 June, 1589, M.A. 4 July, 1593, rector of West Tytherley, Hants, 1596, and of Ardingley, Sussex, 1605. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Harris, Joseph (Harrys), of Cornwall, arm. University Coll., matric. 30 July, 1585, aged 16; student of Middle Temple, 1586, as 2nd son of John, of Lanrest, Cornwall, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.
Basill, Edward, of Devon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 21; one of these names rector of St. Edmund-on-the-Bridge in Exeter 1593. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Waterhouse, Jonas, of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. 30 April, 1585, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1595, as of Datche, Yorks, late of Clifford's Inn, gent.
Bishop, John (Byshop), of London, gent. University Coll., matric. 18 June, 1585, aged 15; called Bishoppe in Mat. Reg.
Brudenell, Edmund (Brudnell), of Bucks. gent. University Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1585, aged 15. See Roger same date.
Brudenell, Roger (Brudnell), of Bucks, gent. University Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1585, aged 14. See Edmund same date.
Flathers, Major, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 21; B.A. 27 Feb., 1589-90.
Griffith, Owen (Gruffith) de "Bang." (? of Bangor), pleb. University Coll., matric. 8 Oct., 1585, aged 34.
Heyney, John, of Devon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 18 March, 1585-6, aged 13 (subs. Heineus).
Basill, Edward, of Devon, 'Gen. Cond.' University Coll., matric. 30 April, 1585, aged 23.
Daunte, John, of co. Gloucester, gent. University Coll., matric. 23 April, 1585, aged 15.
Waterhouse, Henry, of Herts, arm. University Coll., matric. entry 2 July, 1585, aged 13.
Waterhouse, Richard, of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. 30 April, 1585, aged 16.
Dobson, John, of London, gent. University Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 15.

Brooke, John, of York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1586, aged 17; B.A. 1 July, 1590, M.A. 8 May, 1593, B.D. 17 May, 1602, sup. for licence to preach 3 Nov., 1604, D.D. 16 July, 1612, rector of Elmley, Yorks, 1603, vicar of Silkstone 1610, rector of Baynton 1615, canon of York 1613, of Southwell 1614, precentor of York 1615, until his death 23 March, 1616-17, aged 49, father of John 1626. See Le Neve, iii. 156; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Throckmorton, John, of co. Gloucester, equitis fil. University Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1586, aged 14; perhaps M.P. co. Gloucester 1601, and (May), 1604-11; brother of William 1594. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lit(t)leton, Gilbert, of co. Worcester, arm. University Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 16 (son of Gilbert, of Frankley); died unmarried; perhaps brother of Humphrey and John 1576.
Davies, Anthony (Davyes), of London, gent. University Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1586, aged 16 (B.C.L. from All Souls' Coll. 11 July, 1598, and sup. for D.C.L. in 1609 as Davis).
Gerrald, Patrick, of "Com. Culkenny," pleb. University Coll., matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 21; called "Garrat" in Mat. Reg.
Thomas, Leyson, of co. Hereford, gent. University Coll., matric. 6 May, 1586, aged 17; perhaps father of Michael 1611.
Griffith, Peirs (Gruffyth), of co. Carnarvon, militis fil. University Coll., matric. 15 April, 1586, aged 17.
Penry, Richard, of co. Glamorgan, gent. University Coll., matric. entry 1 March, 1586-7, aged 13.
Bryn, Thomas, of 'Com Culkenny,' pleb. University Coll., matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 21.
Astbrooke, John, of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 April, 1586, aged 23.
Warde, William, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1586, aged 19.
Cowth, John, of Dorset, gent. University Coll., matric. 15 April, 1586, aged 15.
Stanton, John (Staunton), of Salop, gent. University Coll., matric. 6 May, 1586.
Deane, Robert, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 3 June, 1586, aged 16.

Woodward, Richard, of co. Warwick, gent. University Coll., matric. 15 Jan., 1587-8, aged 15; perhaps student of Middle Temple 1597, as 2s. Richard, of Stratford-on-Avon, co. Warwick, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hartwell, Robert, of Northants, arm. University Coll., matric. 12 April, 1587, aged 17; one of these names knighted 9 July, 1603.
Simcotes, Thomas (Symcotes), of co. Cambridge, gent. University Coll., matric. 12 April, 1587, aged 15.
Symonds, Robert, of co. Worcester, gent. University Coll., matric. entry 1 March, 1587-8, aged 14.
Kendall, Francis, of co. Derby, gent. University Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1587, aged 19.

Calverley, (Sir) John, of co. Durham. University Coll., matric. 15 June, 1588, aged 17 (as arm.); of Eryholme, co. Durham (only son of Thomas, of Littleburne, chancellor of Durham), a student of Lincoln's Inn 1590, knighted 23 April, 1617, died 12 May, 1638, buried at Brancepeth 13th, father of Timothy and of the next named. See Foster's Yorkshire Collections.

Gale, Thomas, of Devon, arm. University Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1588, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1600 (son of George, of Crediton, Devon, bar.-at-law); died at Crediton 30 April, 1625. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Davies, Tobias, of Bristol, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1588, aged 15; B.A. 15 Nov., 1593, M.A. 10 June, 1596, rector of Chipstable, Somerset, 1597, and of Exford 1612. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Copleston, Humphrey, of Devon, arm. University Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1588, aged 17; sup. for B.A. 13 Dec., 1591, probably of Instow, or Wear Gifford, Devon. See Vivian's Devon Visitations, 227, 232.
Caunton, John, of Kent, gent. University Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1588, aged 15; perhaps of the Middle Temple 1598, as son and heir of Thomas, of Nonnington, Kent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Smithe, Renoulde (Smythe), of co. Derby, pleb. University Coll., matric. 28 June, 1588, aged 20, B.A. 6 Nov., 1592; perhaps student of Gray's Inn 1585. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Noyes, William, of Wilts, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1588, aged 20; B.A. 31 May, 1592, rector of Choldrington, Wilts, 1602. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Mattingley, Humphrey, of Berks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1588, aged 16; demy Magdalen Coll. 1590-5, B.A. 8 Feb., 1593-4. See Bloxam, iv. 231.
Townley, Bernard, of co. Lancaster, pleb. University Coll. matric. 15 Nov., 1588, aged 18; student of Gray's Inn 1591. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Dande, William, of co. Derby, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1588, aged 18; B.A. from Merton Coll. 20 Feb., 1592-3.
Grove, John, of Hants, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1588, aged 15; B.A. from Merton Coll. 14 June, 1592.
Woodwarde, John, of co. Warwick, gent. University Coll., matric. 3 May, 1588, aged 16.
Benson, John, of Northants, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 Jan., 1589-90, aged 16; called Joseph in Mat. Reg.

Rogers, Rufus, of Sussex, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry 23 Oct., 1590, aged 13; B.A. 4 June, 1594, M.A. 7 July, 1597; of St. Stephen, Coleman Street, London, clerk, licenced 19 Feb., 1595-6, to marry Elizabeth, daughter of Thomas Dingley, late of Southampton, gent., deceased; rector of Canterbury St. Peter 1605, and of Fawkenhurst, Kent, 1605. See Foster's Index Eccl. & London Marriage Licences, ed Foster.

Burnand, Edward, of co. Stafford, pleb. University Coll., matric. 2 July, 1590, aged 17; B.A. 4 June, 1594, rector of Avington, Berks, 1604, father of the last named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Scot, Christopher, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 22, B.A. 6 Feb., 1592-3, M.A. 5 Dec., 1598; perhaps rector of Chastleton, Oxon, 1592. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Shrewesbury, Thomas, of Northants. University Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 18, B.A. 15 July, 1596, M.A. 5 July, 1609; rector of Alderley, co. Gloucester, 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Downe, John, of Devon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 24; B.A. 4 June, 1594, one of these names vicar of South Newton, Wilts, 1602. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Reed, Giles, of co. Worcester, arm. University Coll., matric. 14 May, 1590, aged 15; student of Inner Temple 1592, as of Mytton, co. Worcester. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Forster, Francis, of Salop, gent. University Coll., matric. 17 July, 1590, aged 19; student of Lincoln's Inn 1590. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Allen, Walter, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll, matric. 2 July, 1590, aged 18; B.A. 4 June, 1594, M.A. 7 July, 1597.
Carlile, Jonathan, s. Fr., of Bishopton, co. York, p.p. University Coll., matric. 6 Feb., 1590-1, aged 19.
Warwicke, John, of Northants, pleb. University Coll., matric. — Nov., 1590, aged 28.
Gate, Nathaniel, of Oxon, gent. University Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1590, aged 10.
Gate, John, of Essex, gent. University Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1590, aged 12.
Gate, Peter, of Oxon, gent. University Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1590, aged 11.

Gate, Timothy, of Oxon, gent. University Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 12, B.A. 23 Feb., 1596-7; M.A. from Balliol Coll. 2 Dec., 1601, B.D. 24 Oct., 1611 (as Gates), rector of Eaton Hastings, Berks, 1611, of Elkstone, 1611, and of Bishops Cleve, co. Gloucester, 1612. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Coldcoll, Arthur, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 16; B.A. 4 June, 1594, M.A. 22 April, 1597, licenced to preach 9 July, 1606, rector of Plumpton, Northants, 1604, vicar of Arncliffe, Yorks, 1608. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Burdett, William, of Berks, gent. University Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 17; B.A. 15 Nov., 1593, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1603, as 2nd son of Humphrey, late of Sunning, Berks, esq., see John same date. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Agard, Stephen, of Northants, gent. University Coll., matric. 8 July, 1591, aged 15; B.A. 3 July, 1594, son and heir of Ambrose, of Broughton, Northants, esquire, admitted to the Middle Temple 1596. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Woodd, Richard, of co. Gloucester, gent. University Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 16, and B.A. from Christ Church 5 Feb., 1596-7; and of Brookthorp, co. Gloucester; died about 1635, aged 63, and father of Robert 1628.
Gifford, John (Gyfforde), of Devon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 12; probably of Halsbury (s. John of same); died 19 Feb., 1624-5, buried at Parkham.
Goddarde, Richard, of Hants, gent. University Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 15; student of Lincoln's Inn 1594; brother of Edward 1589. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Kenythorp, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, pleb. University Coll., matric. 23 July, 1591, aged 17; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 28 Feb., 1594-5, as "Kenethorpe."
Palmes, Francis, of Hants, gent. University Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 19; perhaps brother of Thomas 1598.
Batte, Thomas (? Butte), of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 18; B.A. 10 June, 1596.
Fradishame, Edward, of Dorset, pleb. University Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 16; B.A. 28 Feb., 1594-5.
Burdett, John, of Berks, gent. University Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 19; see William, same date.
Meredith, Richard, of co. Anglesey, pleb. University Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18.
Pape, John, of co. Lincoln, pleb. University Coll., matric. 23 July, 1591, aged 17.

Bulstrod, Henry, of Bucks, gent. University Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 13; a student of the Inner Temple 1595, as of Hedgerley, Bucks (son of Edward), M.P. Helston 1614, Bucks 1625, died in Jan., 1632, though Wood states that when he was about 60 years of age he was governor of Ailesbury for the parliament 1643 (?), brother of Edward 1604, and father of the next named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Ath., iii. 472.

Grey, Thomas, baronis fil. University Coll., matric. 20 March, 1592-3, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1592, as son and heir apparent of (Arthur) Lord Grey de Wilton; 15th baron 1597-1601, knighted at Dublin 12 July, 1599, attainted for complicity in "Raleigh's conspiracy"; died in the Tower of London 6 July, 1614; brother of William 1604. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Doylie, (Sir) Cope, of Oxon. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1592, aged 18 (as arm.); of Chiselhampton, Oxon (s. John), admitted to Lincoln's Inn 1594, knighted at Greenland, Bucks, 22 Aug., 1627, died 4 Aug., 1633, brother of John same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Doyley, John (Doylie), of Oxon, arm. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1592, aged 14; of Lincoln's Inn 1596, migrated to Gray's Inn, 1606, then of Chiselhampton, Oxon, esq., perhaps M.P. Oxon 1604-11, son of Sir Robert, brother of Cope, 1592. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Radcliffe, Jonas, of co. Lancaster, gent. University Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1592, aged 22; B.A. 22 Feb., 1594-5, fellow, M.A. 6 July, 1598, B.D. 22 March, 1614-15; died 17 Aug., 1626, buried in the college chapel; will at Oxford proved 18 Nov., 1626. See Gutch, i. 63.

Greenwood, Charles (Grenewoode), of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. 2 June, 1592, aged 18; B.A. 16 Feb., 1595-6, fellow, M.A. 5 Dec., 1598, proctor 1609, rector of Thornhill, Yorks, 1613, a benefactor to his college. See Gutch, i. 49; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Gryme, Lancelott, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1592, aged 16; B.A. 23 Feb., 1596-7, M.A. 16 June, 1600, rector of Almsford, Somerset, 1609. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Taverner, Richard, of Middlesex. University Coll., matric. entry 8 Dec., 1592, aged 12; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 25 Feb., 1597-8; student of Gray's Inn 1599. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Chambers, Humfrey, of co. Gloucester, pleb. University Coll., matric. 2 June, 1592, aged 18; B.A. 16 Feb., 1595-6, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1606. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Clarke, Henry, of Hants, arm. University Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1592, aged 16; of Gray's Inn 1594, as son and heir of Thomas of Arlington, Southants, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Hussey, Richard, of Salop, arm. University Coll., matric. 20 March, 1592-3, aged 20; one of these names knighted 18 Dec., 1604; buried 10 Jan., 1628-9.
Lygh, Christopher, of co. Gloucester, cler. fil. University Coll., matric. 2 June, 1592, aged 18; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 21 Feb., 1595-6.
Harrison, James (Harryson), of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1592, aged 14; B.A. 23 Feb., 1596-7, M.A. 16 June, 1600.
Jones, William (Johns), of co. Carnarvon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1592, aged 16; B.A. 21 Feb., 1595-6.
Newman, John, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1592, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 5 Dec., 1597.
Pickering, Antony (Pykerynge), of Hants, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry 20 Oct., 1592, aged 13.
Symons, Edmund (Simons), of Oxon, gent. University Coll., matric. entry 1 Dec., 1592, aged 14.
Durant, Robert (Durrant), of Sussex, pleb. University Coll., matric. 2 June, 1592, aged 18.
Beeche, Humphrey, of Northants, pleb. University Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1592, aged 18.
Carpenter, Giles, of Devon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 20 March, 1592-3, aged 40.
Ortonne, James, of Cheshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 18.

Middlemore, Robert, of Herts, arm. University Coll., matric. 15 Feb., 1593-4, aged 14; of Enfield, Middlesex (son and heir of Henry, one of her majesty's privy chamber), licenced 24 Oct., 1601, to marry Dorothy Fulstone. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Greenewood, George, of Oxon, gent. University Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 15; student of Inner Temple 1596, as of Chastleton, Oxon (s. Thomas); died March, 1650; brother of Thomas 1590, and father of the next. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hales, Stephen, of co. Warwick, arm. University Coll., matric. 25 May, 1593, aged 17, of Newland, co. Warwick; student of Gray's Inn 1592, as eldest son of Charles, reader. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Stockdale, Gregory, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 6 July, 1593, aged 26, B.A. 23 Feb., 1596-7.
Holdsworth, Joshua (Houldsworthe), of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. — Jan., 1593-4, aged 25.
Yardley, John (Yerdeley), of Cheshire, gent. University Coll., matric. 25 June, 1593, aged 25.
Waterhouse, Vincent, of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. 25 June, 1593, aged 18.
Reade, Edward, of Northants, arm. University Coll., matric. 25 June, 1593, aged 15.
Smithe, John, of Dorset, pleb. University Coll., matric. 28 June, 1593, aged 18.

Drewe, (Sir) Thomas, of Devon. University Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 16 (as arm.); B.A. from Trinity Coll. 16 Nov., 1597, of the Inner Temple 1593 (as son and heir of Edward, serjt.-at-law), of the Grange, Broadhembury, Devon, sheriff 1613, knighted 23 July, 1603, buried 15 July, 1651. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Boys, John (Bois), of Kent, arm. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 11, B.A. 10 Dec., 1597, M.A. 16 June, 1600; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1604, B.C.L. 14 Dec., 1609, one of these names rector of Hollingbourne, Kent, 1603, admon. bond (at Oxford) 11 Nov., 1611. See Fasti, i. 276.

Beeley, William, of Beds, arm. University Coll., matric. 31 May, 1594, aged 15, B.A. 8 Feb., 1597-8; M.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 13 Dec., 1602, B.D. 27 March, 1610, rector of Kings Stanley, co. Gloucester, 1615, and archdeacon of Carmarthen 1615. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Ewens, John, of Somerset, gent. University Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 16; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 11 Feb., 1597-8, student of Middle Temple 1593, as son and heir of John of Suddover in Wincanton, Somerset, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Cox, George, of co. Gloucester, gent. University Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 14 (called Cope in Mat. Reg.); B.A. from St. John's Coll. 24 May, 1598, M.A. 19 May, 1601. See Griffith's List of Oxford Wills, 1624.

Jones, Maurice, of co. Carnarv., pleb. University Coll., matric. 14 June, 1594, aged 16; perhaps B.A. from Christ Church 16 Dec., 1602; memo. M.J. of St. Peter's-in-the-East; will at Oxford proved 9 April, 1606.
Osborne, John, of Northants, pleb. University Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1594-5, aged 17; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 10 Dec., 1599.
Hooke, Richard, of co. Hereford, pleb. University Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1594-5, aged 21; B.A. 17 Feb., 1598-9.
Duncombe, John (Doncombe), of Bucks, gent. University Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 14.
Horseman, Christopher, of Oxon, gent. University Coll., matric. 12 July, 1594, aged 14.
Bower, Lawrence, of co. Derby, pleb. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 14.
Turner, William, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 12 July, 1594, aged 17.

Gillingham, Richard, of Dorset, pleb. University Coll., matric. March, 1595-6, aged 18; B.A. 25 Oct., 1599, rector of Lillington, Dorset, 1618, and of Thornford 1623. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Baldwin, John, of Berks, 'cler. fil,' University Coll., matric. 31 Oct., 1595, aged 11; B.A. 23 Feb., 1596-7, vicar of Aldermaston, Berks, 1613. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Parsons, Thomas (Persons), of Dorset, pleb. University Coll., matric. — March, 1595-6, aged 17; one of these names vicar of Stock Gaylard, Dorset, 1599. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Herberte, (Sir) Edward, of co. Montgomery, arm. fil.; University Coll., matric. June, 1596, aged 14; (s. Richard, by his wife Margaret Newport, and born at Eyton, Salop), K.B. 24 July, 1603, ambassador to Louis XIII. of France, M.P. co. Merioneth 1604-11, created Baron Herbert of Cherbury 7 May, 1629, and of Castle Island in Ireland, 31 Dec., died 6, and buried in St.-Giles-inthe-Fields 20 Aug., 1648; brother of Walter 1611. See Ath. iii. 239; & D.N.B.

Barker, John, of Salop, arm. University Coll., matric. — June, 1596, aged 16; B.A. 10 Dec., 1597, subscribed as 'Berker,' a student of Gray's Inn 1599 (as son and heir of Rowland, of Hormond, Salop, arm.), M.P. Shrewsbury 1601, baptized at Uffington 1579, o.s.p. 1618, brother of Walter 1599. See Foster's Gray's Inn Admissions.

Woodbridge, John, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll., matric. entry 23 July, 1596, aged 14, B.A. 4 March, 1602-3, M.A. 11 July, 1606; rector of Stanton Fitzwarren, Wilts, 1625; see Foster's Index Eccl.; for an account of his son John, see Calamy, i. 292; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Haslewood, Edward (Hasselwood), of Northants, arm. University Coll., matric. June, 1596, aged 14; of Maidwell, Northants, died 1615; father of Anthony 1616.
Haslewood, Edmund (Hasselwood), of Northants, arm. University Coll., matric. June, 1596, aged 16; died 5 Aug., 1598; brother of the next-named.
Holdsworth, Henry (Hauldesworth), of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 15; B.A. from Merton Coll. 16 Dec., 1601.
Caldwall, William, of co. Stafford, pleb. University Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 17; B.A. 13 Nov., 1598.
Rider, William, of Bristol, cler. fil. University Coll., matric. — May, 1596, aged 17; B.A. 23 Feb., 1596-7.
Greenwood, James (Grenewood), of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. — May, 1596, aged 17.
Bryerley, Richard, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. Jan., 1596-7, aged 28.
Edwardes, Abraham, of Sussex, pleb. University Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 16.
Porter, Thomas, of Sussex, pleb. University Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 16.

Bridgman, Simon, of Devon, gent. University Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1597, aged 17; B.A. 1 July, 1601, a student of the Inner Temple 1604, late of Lyon's Inn, gent, as of Exeter (son of Jasper, registrar of the archdeaconry of Exeter), died April, 1646, brother of George 1608. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Boys, Edward, of Kent, arm. University Coll., matric. 27 May, 1597, aged 15; of Betshanger, a student of the Middle Temple 1579, as son and heir of Vincent, of Beakesborne, Kent, gent., deceased, died 23 Oct., 1649. See John 1594 of same College. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Allansone, Stephen, of Yorkshire, pleb. University Coll., matric. Nov., 1597, aged 16, B.A. 21 Feb., 1600-1; M.A. from Brasenose Coll. 7 July, 1608, B.D. 8 July, 1619, licence to preach 4 Dec., 1620, rector of Oving, Bucks, 1616. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bulkley, Gabriel, of Anglesea, pleb. University Coll., matric. Oct., 1597, aged 19; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 5 March, 1602-3, M.A. 11 July, 1606.
Colman, Henry, of Kent, pleb. University Coll., matric. Nov., 1597, aged 18.

Webb, George, of Wilts, cler. fil. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 17; scholar Corpus Christi Coll., B.A. 13 Feb., 1601-2, M.A. 23 June, 1605, then in orders, B.D. 11 July, 1618, D.D. 17 Dec., 1623; (son of Hugh, rector of Bromham, Wilts), vicar of Steeple Ashton, Wilts, 1605, rector of SS. Peter and Paul, Bath, 1621-34, chaplain in ordinary to Charles I., bishop of Limerick 1634; died in Limerick Castle 23 June, (or Jan.), 1641; father of Theophilus. See Ath. iii. 29; Cotton's Fasti Ecc. Hib. i. 384; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Tufton, (Sir) Humphrey, of Kent, arm. fil. University Coll., matric. entry 30 June, 1598, aged 14; of the Mote, Maidstone, student of Inner Temple 1601, as of Hothfield, Kent, gent. (son of Sir John); knighted 18 Jan., 1613-14, and created a baronet 24 Dec., 1641, M.P. Maidstone 1640 (L.P.), till secluded in Dec., 1648; died in Oct., 1659, aged 76; brother of John 1591, etc. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Savile, (Sir) George, of Yorks, equitis fil. University Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 15; B.A. 8 Feb., 1600-1; of Sothill, Yorks, student of the Inner Temple 1606 (then a knight), knighted 11 May, 1603 (s. Sir George, knight and bart.), M.P. Morpeth 1601, Appleby 1614; buried at Thornhill 24 Aug., 1614; father of the next, and of William 1626. See Foster's Yorkshire Collection.

Powell, Hugh, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 19; perhaps B.A. from Jesus Coll. 8 May, 1599, M.A. 6 July, 1608, then in orders, and perhaps created B.D. 1 or 2 Nov., 1642, rector of Kelybebil 1600, vicar of Pendoylan 1604, rector of Llanmadoc 1613, of Port Eynon 1613, and of St. Fagans 1614, all co. Glamorgan. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Salusburi, Paul, of Oxon, cler. fil. University Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 18, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 11 Feb., 1601-2; rector of Lillingston Lovell, Oxon, 1605, until 1647, when he resigned from "great age and other infirmities." See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671.

Morgan, Cadwallader, pleb. University Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, B.A. 10 July, 1601 (one of these names incorporated as M.A. of Cambridge 14 July, 1607); possibly vicar of Machynlleth 1607-16, another rector of St. Benet Sherehog, London, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Tufton, Richard, of Kent, arm. University Coll., matric. entry 30 June, 1598, aged 13; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1609, M.P. Grantham 1614, and Rye 1628-9; died 4 Oct., 1631; brother of John 1591, etc. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Galton, John, of Dorset, pleb. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 18; B.A. 24 Nov., 1601, M.A. 31 Jan., 1605-6, chaplain of New College, vicar of East Lulworth, Dorset, 1610, of Combe Keynes 1611. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lee, Joseph, of co. Leicester, cler. fil. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 18; B.A. from Merton Coll. 16 Dec., 1601; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 4 July, 1604, rector of Cathorpe, co. Leicester; father of the next-named.
Clifton, Laurence, of Surrey, pleb. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 16; of the Middle Temple 1600, as only son of Henry, of Guildford, Surrey, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Repington, Edward, of co. Warwick, arm. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 18, B.A. 24 Nov., 1601; perhaps 5s. Thomas, of Amington, co. Warwick; brother of Humphrey and of John 1574.
Courte, Thomas, of Somerset, gent. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 13; perhaps of the Middle Temple 1595, as 2nd son of John, of Bristol, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Boron, Angell, of Middlesex, pleb. University Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1598, aged 16; B.A. 12 May, 1602, M.A. 6 July, 1605, then in orders. See O.H.S., xii. 234.
Lysley, John, of co. Warwick, arm. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 18; perhaps rector of Hartlebury, co. Worcester, 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Walter, Charles, of co. Monmouth, gent. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 16. See History of Monmouthshire, by D. Williams, p. 197, app.
Robinson, Herbert, of co. Carnarvon, gent. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 17.
Cooley, John, of Wilts, 'cler. fil.' University Coll., matric. 27 Oct., 1598, aged 18.
Spring, Thomas, of Norfolk, gent. University Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 15.
Tuchiner, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. University Coll., matric. 27 Oct., 1598, aged 17.
Harris, Thomas, of Sussex, pleb. University Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 19.
Stanesby, John, of Wilts, gent. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1598, aged 17.

Barker, Walter, of Salop, arm. University Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1599, aged 17; a student of Gray's Inn 1601 (as son of Rowland, of Hormond, Salop, arm.), sheriff of Salop 1621, father of Rowland 1640, and brother of John 1596. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Delavale, Claudius, of Northumberland, gent. University Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1599, aged 16; B.A. 8 June, 1603 (as Claude), of the Inner Temple 1605, as of Seaton Delavall, N.B.), brother of Arthur and Francis. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Delavale, Francis, of Northumberland, gent. University Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1599, aged 15; of the Middle Temple 1602 (as 3rd son of Robert, of Seaton Delavall, N.B., etc.), brother of Arthur and Claudius. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Denn, Vincent, of Kent, gent. University Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1599, aged 18; probably son of the preceding and younger brother of John 1579.
Hill, Richard, of co. Warwick, pleb. University Coll., matric. 14 March, 1599-1600, aged 18.

Digges, (Sir) Dudley, of Kent. University Coll., matric. 18 July, 1600, aged 17 (as arm.); B.A. 1 July, 1601, knighted at Whitehall 29 April, 1607, M.P. Tewkesbury April, 1610-11, 1614, 1621-2, 1624-5, 1625-6, Kent 1628-9, gentleman of the privy chamber 1618, a master in chancery 1631-7, bencher, Gray's Inn, 1631, master of the rolls 1638, until his death 18 March, 1639, father of the next and of Thomas 1626. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary; Ath., ii. 634; & D.N.B.

Croke, (Sir) John, of Oxon. University Coll., matric. 18 July, 1600, aged 14 (as arm); of the Inner Temple 1601 (as son and heir of John, recorder of the city of London), of Chilton, Bucks, knighted 30 March, 1609 (or ? 28 June, 1603), M.P. Oxon 1614, Shaftesbury 1628-9, died 10 April, 1640, aged 54, father of William 1636. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Snellinge, Lawrence, of Surrey, pleb. University Coll., matric. — March, 1600-1, aged 18, B.A. 5 July, 1604, M.A. 10 July, 1607, master of Magdalene college school 1610-14, rector of Paul's Cray, Kent, 1614, until deprived 1637, and excommunicated. See Bloxam, iii. 142; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hales, (Sir) John, of co. Warwick, arm. University Coll., matric. 13 Feb., 1600-1, aged 16; student of Gray's Inn 1600 (as son of Charles of Newland in the co. of the city of Coventry), knighted 17 July, 1617, gentleman pensioner to King James, M.P. Queenborough 1628-9. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Delavale, Arthur, of Northumberland, arm. University Coll., matric. 17 Oct., 1600, aged 12; of the Inner Temple 1607, as of Seaton Delaval, N.B., gent., brother of Claudius and Francis here named, all sons of Sir Robert. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Langley, Philip, of Bristol, arm. University Coll., matric. 18 July, 1600, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1602, as son and heir of Philip, late of city of Bristol, esq., deceased. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Manners, Francis, of Notts, equitis fil. University Coll., matric. 13 Feb., 1600-1, aged 17; perhaps a student of the Inner Temple 1602, as of Belvoir Castle, co. Leicester, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Aubrey, William, of co. Brecon, arm. University Coll., matric. 18 July, 1600, aged 16; of New Inn, gent., son of John, of Brecon, esquire, admitted to the Middle Temple 1600. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Rayner, John (Reyner), of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 2 May, 1600, aged 18; B.A. 21 Jan., 1603-4 (his father then dead), fellow, M.A. 18 Nov., 1606; will at Oxford proved 24 June, 1613.
Stanhope, Thomas, of Notts, equitis fil. University Coll., matric. entry 18 July, 1600, aged 14, 3rd son of Sir Thomas, of Shelford, baptised 14 March, 1585; brother of John, and of Edward, 1575.
Roche, Joshua, of Somerset, gent. University Coll., matric. 17 Oct., 1600, aged 21; rector of Hardington, 1617, and vicar of Buckland Dinham, Somerset, 1632. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Foster, John, of Sussex, gent. University Coll., matric. 18 July, 1600, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1611 (as of Iden, Sussex). See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Radcliffe, Savile, of co. Lancaster, gent. University Coll., matric. 17 Oct., 1600, aged 16; of Todmorden (s. Joshua); died 29 Sept., 1652; father of Joshua.
Smithe, John (Smythe), of Southants, gent. University Coll., matric. 18 July, 1600, aged 16, B.A. 8 June, 1603, M.A. 11 July, 1606.
Idle, William, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 18 July, 1600, aged 15, subs. as "Idill."
Cloughe, William, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 18 July, 1600, aged 20.
Coker, Thomas, of Dorset, gent. University Coll., matric. 5 March, 1600-1, aged 17.
Glover, Thomas, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 2 May, 1600, aged 16.
Hammon, George, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 Jan., 1600-1, aged 27.
Duke, Stephen, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 Jan., 1600-1, aged 18.
Jewson, Thomas, of Bucks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 2 May, 1600, aged 16.

Poulett, John, of Somerset, militis fil. University Coll., matric. 12 June, 1601, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1610, as of Hinton, Somerset, gent. (s. Sir Anthony), M.P. Somerset (Oct.) 1610-11, 1614, Lyme Regis 1621-2, created Lord Poulet, of Hinton St. George, 23 June, 1627, knighted 27 Sept., 1635, a distinguished royalist commander; died 20 March, 1649; brother of Amias 1601, and Henry 1603; father of John and Francis 1632, and Amias 1643. See Foster's Peerage.

Saunders, John, of Berks, gent. University Coll., matric. entry 22 May, 1601, aged 11; B.A. 28 Jan., 1607-8; of Wolston; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1616 (as son and heir of Thomas, of Wolston, Berks, gent.), recorder of Reading and M.P. in 5 parliaments 1621-2, 1624-5, 1625, 1626, and 1628-9; died 29 April, 1638. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Lunsford, Thomas, of Sussex, arm. University Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 15; of Wylie in East Hothley, Sussex (son of Sir John); died at Greenwich, where he was buried 4 Nov., 1637; father of Col. Sir Thomas Lunsford, knighted 28 Dec., 1641, lieutenant of the Tower, and died in 1691.

Bunny, Francis, of co. Durham, gent. University Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1601, aged 17; a student of the Inner Temple 1602, as of Ryton, co. Durham, born 9 Nov., 1584, died 26 Feb., 1610, buried at Ryton. See Bloxam, iv. 156; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Bragge, Edmond, of London, gent. University Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1601, aged 16; a student of Gray's Inn 1602 (query: migrated to the Middle Temple in 1603, as son and heir of Roger, of London, gent.). See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Hanson, Robert, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 14; B.A. 9 June, 1605; fellow Magdalen Coll., M.A. 7 May, 1611; son of Nicholas, attorney-at-law, near Elland. See Foster's Yorkshire Collection.

Sal(u)sbury, John, of Devon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 30 Jan., 1601-2, aged 15; B.A. 30 Jan., 1604-5, M.A. 6 July, 1610, as Salsbury; perhaps vicar of Litlington, co. Cambridge, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hannington, Henry, of Dorset, pleb. University Coll., matric. 26 June, 1601, aged 16; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 28 Feb., 1605-6, vicar of Bewfield, Kent, 1610, and of Hougham, Kent, 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Barcrofte, Robert, of co. Lancaster, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 14; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 20 June, 1605, M.A. 18 March, 1608-9, B.D. 18 June, 1617, D.D. 18 July, 1623.
Poulett, Amias, of Somerset, militis fil. University Coll., matric. entry 12 June, 1601, aged 14; B.A. 5 July, 1604, as Paulet; probably son of Sir Anthony, brother of John 1601, and Henry 1603.
Duffield, Cornelius, of York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 17; B.A. 15 March, 1610-11, butler of his college, administration at Oxford (granted) 1 April, 1629.
Goddard, Edward, of Wilts, arm. University Coll., matric. 30 April, 1601, aged 17; of Englesham, s. Richard, of Upham, Wilts, and father of Francis 1632.
Goddard, Thomas, of Wilts, arm. University Coll., matric. 30 April, 1601, aged 18; student of Lincoln's Inn 1604. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Ashton, Samuel, of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1601, aged 19. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Tayler, John, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 5 March, 1601-2, aged 16, B.A. 9 June, 1605.
Chicheley, William, of co. Cambridge, gent. University Coll., matric. 13 Nov., 1601, aged 17.
Darell, William (Dorell), of Sussex, gent. University Coll., matric. 3 July, 1601, aged 14.
Gery, Richard, of co. Cambridge, gent. University Coll., matric. 30 April, 1601, aged 17.
Norris, Robert, of Devon, gent. University Coll., matric. 26 March, 1601, aged 17.
Cary, Robert, of Devon, gent. University Coll., matric. 3 July, 1601, aged 16.

Gate, Thomas, of Oxon, gent. University Coll., matric. 30 April, 1602, aged 12 (as Gate), bar.-atlaw of the Inner Temple 1615 (as Gates, of Churchill, Oxon), bencher 1632, serjeant-at-law 1648, baron of exchequer 1648, until his death 19 Aug., 1650, aged 63, buried in the Temple church 23rd; licenced 2 March, 1621-2 (as Gate) to marry Anne, daughter of Thomas Morley, of St. Lawrence, Old Jewry, London. See Foss' Judges; Foster's Judges and Barristers; & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Symonds, Edward, of Dorset, arm. University Coll., matric. 23 April, 1602, aged 14, B.A. 26 Feb., 1605-6; M.A. from Oriel Coll. 2 July, 1608; vicar of Frampton 1613, and rector of Melbury Abbas, Dorset, 1616, rector of Wanstrow, Somerset, 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bennet, Simon, of London, gent. University Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 18; a student of the Inner Temple 1605. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. [Addenda: perhaps created a baronet 17 July, 1627, died 1631, a benefactor to his college. See Gutch, i. 49.]

Partridge, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 21; B.A. 11 July, 1606, M.A. 7 July, 1609, rector of Little Wormington, co. Gloucester, 1623; perhaps father of James 1688. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Savile, John, of Yorks, arm. fil. nat. max. University Coll., matric. 18 March, 1602-3, aged 16; perhaps bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1612, as son and heir of Francis, of Barrowby, co. Lincoln. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Symonds, Robert, of Dorset, arm. University Coll., matric. 23 April, 1602, aged 15, B.A. 30 Jan., 1604-5; student of Middle Temple 1605, as son and heir of Giles, of Cliffe, Dorset, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Cocks, Thomas, of Kent, gent. University Coll., matric. 11 March, 1602-3, aged 15, B.A. 31 Oct., 1606; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 20 June, 1609, rector of Bonnington, Kent, 1615. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Diston, William, of co. Gloucester, gent. University Coll., matric. 29 Oct., 1602, aged 17; B.A. (sup. 8 March) 1609-10, vicar of Newent, co. Gloucester, 1617. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Holdsworth, Michael (Haldsworth), of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. 9 July, 1602, aged 17.
Sotwell, Edward, of Yorks, gent. University Coll., matric. 18 June, 1602, aged 17.

Leighe, William, of Devon, arm. fil. nat. max. University Coll., matric. 29 July, 1603, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1614 (as son and heir of Thomas, of Northam, Devon, and of Middle Temple, bar.-at-law, see Thomas Ley 1571, page 912). See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Dickson, Robert, of co. York, 'cler. fil.' University Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 16 (called Dixon in Mat. Reg.); B.A. 3 Feb., 1606-7, M.A. 24 Oct., 1609, B.D. 3 April, 1633, one of these names vicar of Haltwhistle, Northumberland, 1616. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Digges, Leonard (or Digs), of London, gent. University Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 15; B.A. 31 Oct., 1606, created M.A. in congregation 20 Nov., 1626 (son of Thomas, of Chilham, Kent), died 7 April, 1635, brother of Sir Dudley. See Fasti, i. 428; & Ath., ii. 592.

Woodwarde, William, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 27 Jan., 1603-4, aged 17, B.A. 27 June, 1607, M.A. 6 July, 1610; vicar of Canterbury St. Dunstan 1614-15, rector of Glimpton, Oxon, 1626. See Add. MS. 15,670, p. 217; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Martine, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. University Coll., matric. 27 Jan., 1603-4, aged 21; B.A. 23 April, 1607, M.A. 18 Jan., 1610-11; perhaps rector of Brimpsfield, co. Gloucester, 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Harris, Hugh, of Devon, gent. University Coll., matric. 15 April, 1603, aged 14; B.A. 11 July, 1606, M.A. 27 June, 1609, vicar of Chittlehampton, Devon, 1620. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wilbee, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 April, 1603, aged 18 (subscribes "Wilke"), B.A. 31 Oct., 1606, M.A. 3 July, 1609.
Meggs, Henry, of Dorset, gent. University Coll., matric. 27 Jan., 1603-4, aged 18; of Bradford Peverell, Dorset (s. Peverell); died in 1656.
Basset, William, of Devon, eq. fil. University Coll., matric. 29 July, 1603, aged 20; son of Sir Arthur, and brother of Robert 1589.
Poulett, Henry, of Somerset, militis fil. University Coll., matric. entry 13 May, 1603, aged 13; brother of Amias, and John 1601.
Battye, John, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 April, 1603, aged 18; B.A. 31 Oct., 1606, M.A. 27 June, 1609.
Baker, William, of Devon, gent. University Coll., matric. 29 July, 1603. aged 16; B.A. 3 Feb., 1606-7, M.A. 24 Oct., 1609.
Tingle, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 10 Feb., 1603-4, aged 19, B.A. 11 July, 1606, M.A. 6 July, 1610.
Blundell, Richard, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 24 Feb., 1603-4, aged 19; B.A. 30 June, 1606.
Clerke, Henry, of co. Southampton, arm. University Coll., matric. 15 April, 1603, aged 16.
Aspinall, Brian, of co. Lancaster, pleb. University Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 15.
Rayer, Thomas, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 3 June, 1603, aged 18.

Lister, (Sir) John (Lyster), of Yorks, gen. fil. University Coll., matric. 13 July, 1604, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1606 (as son and heir of John, of Kingston-upon-Hull, Yorks, gent.), knighted 23 May, 1628, twice mayor of Kingston-upon-Hull and M.P. 1621-2, 1624-5, 1625, 1626, 1628-9, April —May, 1640, and 1640 (l.p.), until his death 23 Dec., 1640. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Saxton, William, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 17; perhaps B.A. from Catherine Hall, Cambridge, 1608-9, M.A. 1612, vicar of Gringley, Notts, 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Dancaster, Anthony, s. A., of co. Southants, 'famulus Dri. Abbott vic. Can.' University Coll., matric. 4 May, 1604, aged 20.
Towne, William, of co. Lancaster, pleb. University Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1604, aged 18.
Longbotham, Richard, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 27 April, 1604, aged 17.
Fettiplace, Edmund, of Berks, arm. University Coll., matric. 8 March, 1604-5, aged 16.
Fettiplace, John, of Berks, arm. University Coll., matric. 8 March, 1604-5, aged 16.

Wynne, Morgan, of co. Denbigh, gent. University Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1605, aged 20, B.A. 23 June, 1609; fellow All Souls' Coll., M.A. 23 Feb., 1612-13 (incorporated at Cambridge 1614), B.D. 14 July, 1620, D.D. 26 March, 1634; sinecure rector of Cilcain 1622, and of Llanrwst, co. Denbigh, 1624, rector of Stilton, Hunts, 1622, canon 1623, and archdeacon of Lincoln 1627; rector of Symondsbury, Dorset, 1623-4, Cranford St. John, Northants, Jan.-April, 1625, Braxted, Kent, 1625-39, and of Scotter, co. Lincoln, 1640. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Lansdowne MS. 985, ff. 74, 77; & Thomas, 565.

Ancketyll, Henry, of Dorset, arm. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1605, aged 17, B.A. 19 June, 1609, M.A. 10 July, 1612; an original fellow of Wadham Coll. 1613-21, created D.D. 16 Jan., 1642-3, rect or of Mells 1620, and of Clapton, Somerset, 1635-42, preb. of Bath and Wells, 1639, vicar East Harptree 1640, 'Col. Anketell, the priest or malignant doctor,' Royalist Governor of Corfe Castle. See Gardiner, 8; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Bunny, George, of co. York, arm. University Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1605, aged 18; B.A. 20 Feb., 1608-9, M.A. 6 July, 1611, a student of Lincoln's Inn 1611 (as son of Richard, of Newland, Yorks, esq.), heir of Francis first named, made his will 11 Aug., 1668. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Routh, John, of Yorks, arm. University Coll., matric. 7 Feb., 1605-6, aged 19; student of Middle Temple 1608, as son and heir of Richard, of Pollington, Yorks, gent.; brother of Peter. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Nevill, Gilbert, of Notts, arm. University Coll., matric. 28 June, 1605, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1608, as son and heir of Hercy, of Grove, Notts; father of Anthony 1635. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Laurence, George, of Dorset, gent. University Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1605, aged 16; perhaps of Winterbourne Steepleton, baptised 8 July, 1586; brother of Charles 1610, and father of Richard 1636.
Washington, Philip, of Yorks, arm. University Coll., matric. entry 13 Dec., 1605, aged 14, B.A. 23 June, 1609, fellow, M.A. 10 July, 1612; died 6 April, 1635; brother of Darcy. See Gutch, i. 66.
Cornishe, Michael, of Oxon, pleb. University Coll., matric. 28 June, 1605, aged 18; B.A. 16 May, 1607, vicar of (a portion) of Bampton, Oxon, 1614. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Saunders, William, of Bucks, gent. University Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1605, aged 15; student of Inner Temple 1608, as of Wavendon, Bucks, gen. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Routh, Peter, of Yorks, arm. University Coll., matric. 7 Feb., 1605-6, aged 16; brother of John 1606. See Foster's Yorkshire Visitations, 570.
Washington, Darcy, of Yorks, arm. University Coll., matric. 13 Dec., 1605, aged 16; brother of Philip.
Frebodye, John, of Sussex, pleb. University Coll., matric. 28 June, 1605, aged 19.

Stede, William, of Kent, militis fil. University Coll., matric. entry 4 July, 1606, aged 12, B.A. 6 Nov., 1609; B.C.L. from All Souls' Coll. 22 Nov., 1615, D.C.L. 27 Nov., 1621; (s. Sir William, of Harrietsham), an advocate of Doctors' Commons 1623, official of the court of Canterbury; licenced 17 Dec., 1632, to marry Hester, daughter of Sir Edwin Sandys, late of Eaton, Beds, knight, and his wife Elizabeth baroness Sandys; brother of John 1606, and Thomas 1610. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & Coote's Civilians, 75.

Hilton, Francis, of co. Durham, arm. University Coll., matric. 30 Jan., 1606-7, aged 15; B.A. 15 April, 1611, M.A. 8 July, 1614 (4th son Thomas, of Hilton Castle, co. Durham); rector of Kirkhaugh, Northumberland, died unmarried; brother of the next.

Musgrave, Richard, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 31 Oct., 1606, aged 17, B.A. 15 Feb., 1609-10; one of these names a student of Gray's Inn 1601, as of Hartley, Westmorland, but query, see William 1610. See Foster's Gray's Inn Register.

Radcliffe, Thomas, of Yorks, arm. University Coll., matric. 4 July, 1606, aged 17; B.A. 23 March, 1609-10, M.A. 10 July, 1612, fellow 1613-1648, restored in 1660, in which year he died; brother of George 1609. See Burrows, 555.

Habergham, Matthew, of co. Lancaster, pleb. University Coll., matric. 30 Jan., 1606-7, aged 18; a student of Gray's Inn 1610, as son and heir of Laurence, of Habergam, co. Lancaster. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Stede, (Sir) John, of Kent, militis fil. University Coll., matric. 4 July, 1606, aged 15, B.A. 14 Oct., 1608; of Harrietsham (s. Sir William), knighted 4 March, 1614; brother of Thomas 1610, and of William 1606.

Remingtoun, Henry, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 31 Oct. 1606, aged 19, B.A. 21 Jan., 1607-8, M.A. from St. Alban Hall 10 Dec., 1612, rector of Lockington, Yorks, 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Stone, Callingewood, of co. Derby, cler. fil. University Coll., matric. 4 July, 1606, aged 20.
Nayler, Ambrose, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 30 Jan., 1606-7, aged 19.
Willett, John, University Coll., matric. 11 July, 1606, aged 30, "serviens coll."

Holmes, Christopher, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 8 May, 1607, aged 17 (subs. as Hollnes); B.A. 22 Nov., 1610, M.A. 9 July, 1613, vicar of Clanfield, Oxon, 1615. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Radcliffe, (Sir) George (Ratcliffe), of Yorks, arm. fil. University Coll., matric. 3 Nov., 1609, aged 15; B.A. 24 May, 1612; created D.C.L. 31 Oct., 1643; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1618, and a bencher 1632, as of Thornhill, Yorks, gent.; perhaps brother of Savile and of Thomas 1606. See Fasti, ii. 63; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Fettiplace, William, of Berks, arm. University Coll., matric. 7 April, 1609, aged 17; B.A. 3 Nov., 1612 (? fellow of Merton Coll. 1613, see O.H.S. iv. 280); bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1620 (as son of Christopher, of Letcombe Regis, 'arm.'). See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Musgrave, William, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 17; one of these names a student of Inner Temple 1613, as of Hayton Castle, Cumberland, but query, see Richard 1606. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Stede, Thomas, of Kent, militis fil. University Coll., matric. entry 16 June, 1610, aged 14, B.A. 3 Nov., 1612; of Basted, Kent (s. Sir William, of Harrietsham); brother of John and William 1606, and father of William 1637.

Waddington, Matthew, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 20, B.A. 18 Jan., 1610-11, M.A. 8 July, 1614; vicar of Chesterfield, co. Derby, 1616. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Whit(e)horne, Thomas, of Somerset, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 21, B.A. 11 Nov., 1611, M.A. 8 July, 1614; rector of Upton Helions, Devon, 1621. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Waddington, Nicholas, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 18, B.A. 6 Feb., 1611-12, M.A. 7 July, 1615; rector of Luckington, Wilts, 1623. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Howgill, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 20; B.A. 15 April, 1611, M.A. 8 July, 1614, vicar of Bingley, Yorks, 1618. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Holland, Timothy, of London, cler. fil. University Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 15, demy Magdalen Coll. 1612-14. See Bloxam, v. 42.
Braye, Henry, of co. Southampton, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 17; B.A. 7 Jan., 1613-14, M.A. 10 July, 1618.
Hulette, John, of Wilts, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 18; B.A. 3 Nov., 1612, M.A. 7 July, 1615.
Faber, Stephen, of co. York, "paup. schol." University Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 17; B.A. 4 March, 1613-14.
Jackson, Philip, of co. Stafford, gent. University Coll., matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 17; B.A. 6 Feb., 1611-12.
Browne, Richard, of co. Derby, gent. University Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 14; B.A. 5 March, 1613-14.
Hulett, Roger, of Wilts, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 17; B.A. 8 Feb., 1612-13.
Bellwood, Mark, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 18.

Forman, Abraham, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov. 1611, aged 17; B.A. 17 Feb., 1614-15, M.A. 20 April, 1618, fellow 1619 (incorporated at Cambridge 1626), B.D. 7 July, 1627, sup. for D.D. 2 May, 1644, expelled from his fellowship in 1648, restored 1660; died 6 July, 1667, aged 75. See Gutch, i. 347; & Burrows, 511.

Nutter, William, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1611, aged 18; B.A. 17 Feb., 1614-15, M.A. 20 April, 1618, perhaps rector of Aston Ingham, co. Hereford, 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Greenacres, John, of Lancashire, arm. University Coll., matric. 5 July, 1611, aged 15; a student of Gray's Inn 1608, as son and heir of Richard of Worston, Lancashire. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Heber, Thomas, of Yorks, arm. University Coll., matric. 5 July, 1611, aged 16; of Marton (s. Thomas of same), a justice of the peace; buried at East Marton 26 Jan., 1659.

Rhodes, Ralphe (Rhoades), of Yorks. pleb. University Coll., matric. 22 May. 1612, aged 18, B.A. 13 May, 1615, vicar of Alfreton 1618-32, and rector of Pinxton, co. Derby, 1629. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Woodrove, Richard, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1612, aged 17, B.A. 4 July, 1616; vicar of Roystone 1619-27, and of Darrington, (both) Yorks, 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Stead, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 3 April, 1612, aged 17, B.A. 9 Nov., 1615, M.A. 10 July, 1618 (as Steede).
Franckline, Philip, of Wilts, pleb. University Coll., matric. 3 April, 1612, aged 16; B.A. 11 July, 1615, M.A. 10 July, 1618.
Rigbie, John, of co. Lancaster. University Coll., matric. 5 Feb., 1612-13, aged 18, "paup. schol.;" B.A. 26 Jan., 1615-16.
Mathew, Andrew, of Wilts, pleb. University Coll., matric. 3 April, 1612, aged 16.

Chambers, Humfrey, of Somerset, gent. University Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1615, aged 16; B.A. 15 Oct., 1618, M.A. 6 July, 1621, B.D. 22 Feb., 1630-1, created D.D. 12 April, 1648, rector of Claverton, Somerset, 1623, one of the assembly of divines, 'and maintained a horse and man at his own charge in actual service against the king,' rector of Strichley, Salop, 1648, rector of Pewsey, Wilts, 1646, 'when the act of uniformity was published he quitted his living and his life together,' buried in Pewsey Church 8 Sept., 1662, 'with no other ceremony than that we would use to a dog.' See Ath., iii. 610; Calamy, iii. 370; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Jemmatt, John, of Berks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1615, aged 18; B.A. 15 Oct., 1618, M.A. 6 July, 1621, vicar of Eastmanstead Cheynies, Bucks, 1642, vicar of St. Giles, Reading, 1648-9; father of John next-named, and perhaps of Samuel 1652. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Moore, John, of Southants, arm. University Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1615, aged 14; student of Lincoln's Inn 1617 (as son and heir of John, of Baddesley, Southants, serjeant-at-law), he or his father M.P. Lymington, Hants, 1624-6. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Smith, William, of Wilts, gent. University Coll., matric. entry 7 July, 1615, aged 14, B.A. 14 July, 1619; perhaps M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 9 May, 1622.
Daniell, Edward, of Wilts, arm. University Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1615, aged 17; B.A. 14 May, 1619.
Hague, Abraham (Haighe), of Yorks, pleb. University Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1615, aged 18.
Griffin, Henry, of Oxon, cler. fil. University Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1615, aged 16.
Pysing, John, of Dorset, pleb. University Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1615, aged 15.

Randolph, Edmund, of Kent, gent. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1616, aged 15; B.A. 1 Feb., 1619-20, M.A. 6 July, 1622, B.Med. and licenced to practice medicine 28 March, 1626, D.Med. Padua, incorporated 3 July, 1628; (son of Bernard, of Biddenden, Kent), practiced in Canterbury, and died 1649, buried in St. George's church; brother of Herbert next-named. See Fasti, i. 443.

Elmehirst, John, of co. York, pleb. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov. 1616, aged 17; B.A. 23 Feb., 1619-20, M.A. 6 July, 1622, fellow 1621, ejected 1651 (s. John, of Lewdine), baptised 7 May, 1598, rector of Aston Ingham, co. Hereford, 1636, vicar of Linton, co. Hereford, 1661; perhaps father of Herbert. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Halsey, Richard, of London, pleb. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1616, aged 16; B.A. 11 Feb., 1619-20, M.A. 6 July, 1622, vicar of East Dean, Sussex, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,670, p. 36.
Plummer, Richard, of Wilts, pleb. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1616, aged 15; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 7 July, 1621; vicar of Great Bedwyn, Wilts, 1627, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Monyns, (Sir) Edward, of Kent, baronetti fil. nat. max. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1616, aged 16; 2nd bart., of Waldershare, Kent, high sheriff 1645; died 1663; brother of Thomas.
Randolph, Harbart, of Kent, gent. University Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1616, aged 16; of Biddenden (s. Bernard); died 8 Sept., 1644; brother of Edmund.
